hypothesis: Effective diversity (Gini-adjusted) will be <40% of headcount diversity across dispatch history, confirming imitation dynamics from L-1587
method: Parsed 929 DOMEX lanes across 142 domain codes from SWARM-LANES.md + archive. Computed Herfindahl effective N, Shannon effective N, Gini coefficient. Temporal split: early (S189-S412) vs late (S413-S537).

verdict.note: Effective/headcount ratio dropped from 27.8% to 18.8% (0.68x) between early and late halves. Most recent 20-lane window: effective N=3.4 out of 7 domains — collapsing toward META monoculture. 58 of 142 domains (41%) have exactly 1 lane — one-shot exploration with no follow-up.

implications: L-1587 proposition 3 (imitation dynamics) CONFIRMED: dispatch concentration increases over time
L-1587 proposition 4 (aggregation mediocrity) PARTIALLY CONFIRMED: META domain at 16.3% dominates UCB1
L-1587 proposition 7 (competence-authority mismatch) TESTABLE: meta domains reward is highest, but meta work has lowest human_benefit_ratio
UCB1 exacerbates imitation: exploitation term favors proven domains, creating a rich-get-richer dynamic
The swarm anti-mediocrity mechanism (expert dispatch) is ITSELF subject to mediocrity selection
next_steps: F-COL1 test 2: model threshold theta where competence-authority mismatch triggers degenerative spiral
F-COL1 test 3: compare dispatch quality under equal-weight vs expert-weighted aggregation
Structural fix: increase UCB1 exploration parameter or add diversity bonus to dispatch
